title: Real Estate Agent Leads at eXp Realty Explained
excerpt: Unlock the secret to high-quality leads with eXp Realty’s Revenos program! Discover how to boost your real estate income with targeted leads.
content:
Key Takeaways: Revenos is eXp Realty’s internal referral and lead division that connects qualified buyers and sellers with trained eXp agents through structured programs. Leads are matched, not sold in bulk, and most programs operate on a pay-at-closing referral model rather than upfront ad spend.

TL;DR About Revenos Leads at eXp Realty

  • Revenos is eXp Realty’s in-house lead and referral division
  • Leads are matched to agents based on experience and certification
  • Programs focus on relocation, affinity groups, military, REO, investors, and sellers
  • Most leads are referral-based with fees paid at closing
  • All training and certifications are included for eXp agents
  • Revenos supplements, not replaces, other eXp lead systems

Real estate agent leads are one of the biggest friction points in the industry. Most agents are forced to choose between expensive paid leads or inconsistent self-generated marketing. eXp Realty takes a different approach by operating an internal division dedicated entirely to sourcing and distributing qualified opportunities to its agents.

Revenos functions as that division. It is not a single lead source, but a structured ecosystem of referral and demand-based programs designed to route real consumers to trained eXp agents.

This article explains how Revenos fits into the broader eXp Realty lead ecosystem available to eXp agents. Here’s your handy dandy index:

Table of Contents

TL;DR About Revenos Leads at eXp Realty
eXp Realty’s Quality Leads
How Revenos Matches Leads to Agents
Revenos Benefits
How to Get Revenos Leads
Revenos Programs Explained
Realtor Leads Pay at Closing
What Agents Also Ask About Revenos Leads
Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty
Frequently Asked Questions
Related eXp Realty Lead Guides

eXp Realty’s Quality Leads

Revenos is eXp Realty’s internal referral and lead distribution division. It operates separately from third-party portals and paid lead vendors by routing opportunities generated through corporate partnerships, relocation programs, affinity relationships, and institutional channels.

Leads distributed through Revenos are not sold in bulk. Instead, they are matched to qualified eXp agents based on program requirements, geographic coverage, and certification status. This structure is designed to route consumer demand already in motion rather than generate speculative inquiries through advertising alone.

Revenos exists to centralize brokerage-level lead sources that would otherwise be fragmented across multiple vendors or unavailable to individual agents.

Revenos offers eight distinct programs under its umbrella. Each program provides a specific type of lead, allowing agents to choose what suits them best. These programs include ExpressOffers, eXp Affinity, eXp Relocation, eXp Honor Military Rewards, eXp REO (Real Estate Owned), Institutional Investment Services, Bundle Select, and Making It Rain.

In 2024, Revenos provided thousands of top-quality real estate agent leads, generating over $2 billion in closed sales for eXp agents.

How Revenos Matches Leads to Agents

Revenos programs use predefined eligibility criteria to determine which agents may receive specific lead types. These criteria vary by program and may include licensing tenure, transaction history, certification completion, response standards, or experience with specific transaction categories.

Matching is based on operational fit rather than volume purchasing. Agents do not compete in open rotations or bidding environments. Instead, leads are routed according to program rules designed to align consumer needs with agent preparedness.

This approach allows Revenos to function as a brokerage-level routing system rather than a traditional advertising or lead resale platform.

Revenos Benefits

Revenos programs are available only to eXp Realty agents and operate as part of the brokerage’s internal lead infrastructure. Participation is optional and program access depends on meeting defined requirements.

Most Revenos programs use a referral-fee-at-closing model rather than upfront lead purchases. Referral percentages vary by program and are disclosed in advance. Some programs involve advertising or platform fees, while others do not.

Revenos is intended to supplement an agent’s broader lead strategy rather than replace organic marketing, referrals, or CRM-driven lead generation.

How to Get Revenos Leads

  • Become an eXp Agent: Join eXp Realty.
  • Get Training: Enroll in the Revenos certification training program of your choice to gain the knowledge, confidence, and expertise needed to represent clients exceptionally.  Or, better yet, enroll in all the Revenos certification training programs so you’ll be set to conquer any lead type.
  • Gain Qualifications: Gain the standards preferred by Revenos clients to increase your chances of being chosen for the real estate leads.
  • Note the Gray Area: Even if you don’t meet a client’s preferred qualifications, there are situations when you will still be the chosen agent.  So don’t delay in getting certified for maximum opportunity.

Revenos Programs Explained

Let’s get into the various eXp referral programs and their agent qualifications. Remember, all programs are open to all eXp Agents and all training is completely FREE. And, yes, this is on top of all of the arsenal of other eXp training.

ExpressOffers

ExpressOffers is a platform that aggregates multiple iBuyers (including Opendoor) ready to quickly make cash offers to sellers. eXp agents are assigned these quality leads and can earn commissions from both negotiating terms with the seller and listing the home if the iBuyer puts it back on the market.

  • Certification Format: On-Demand Course
  • Preferred Client Qualifications: ExpressOffers Certification only

eXp Affinity

eXp Affinity partners with large companies who provide various real estate related services to their clients. Revenos forms relationships with these companies to be their source of supplying real estate agents. eXp Affinity currently includes five companies that provide these quality leads to eXp agents.

Certification Format: One Live Class offered once a month

Client Qualifications:  Agents should meet the specific qualifications of the providing company.  Currently those preferred client qualifications start with full-time agents who have been licensed for 2 years and have done 12 transactions in the prior 12 months.

eXp Relocation

eXp Relocation works with corporations to provide services to their employees needing to transfer to a different location. eXp Relocations matches local eXp agents to these corporate transferee clients.

Certification Format: Live courses

Preferred Client Qualifications:  Minimum of one year of experience

eXp Honor Military Rewards

eXp Honor Military Rewards assists active duty, veterans and retired service members from our US armed forces with their real estate needs.

Certification Format: Get Relocations certified in live courses

Preferred Client Qualifications:  Minimum of one year of experience

eXp REO (Real Estate Owned)

eXp agents manage and list REO properties, providing expertise in assisting financial institutions, investors, and government agencies. REO refers to any property that is taken back through either a legal foreclosure, Deed in Lieu of Foreclosure, or any other properties or notes purchased at a court foreclosure auction. REO properties may also be called Corporate Owned, Bank Owned, or Bank Repo properties.

Certification Format: Live Training

Preferred Client Qualifications:  Minimum of two years of REO experience; licensed for at least three years; experience in repair management, renovations, or property management; possess a network of local vendors.

Institutional Investment Services

Institutional Investment Services helps some of the world’s largest landlords invest in or sell single family rental properties by connecting them with top-performing eXp agents for expert support.

Certification Format: live training

Preferred Client Qualifications:  Experience working with investors

eXp Bundle Select Program

Bundle Select offers bundled real estate services to corporations and organizations, saving employees 20% or more on transaction fees and providing 24/7 concierge support.

Revenos provides eXp agents with customized websites and videos to educate corporations and organizations about Bundle Select’s immense value.

This is not a little deal! Currently over 6 Million employees are already on the Bundle Select Platform including Adobe, Visa, Salesforce, Progressive, Perks at Work and more!

Certification Format: One Live Class offered twice per month

Preferred Client Qualifications:  None, but driven by eXp agents with corporate and organization connections.

Additional Perks: eXp agents generating accounts earns a 25% referral fee from all business done through the corporations/organizations they bring into Bundle Select.

Additional Requirements: $1000 yearly subscription fee

Making it Rain

Making it Rain leverages the power of digital ads to build a client database for eXp agents that keeps on giving.  These ads are designed and placed by eXp’s expert staff saving eXp agents time and money.

Certification Format:  On-Demand Courses with prerequisite introductory courses per each lead generation product each taking 45 minutes to complete.

Preferred Client Qualifications:  None

Additional Requirements: eXp agents pay for these cost-effective and affordable ads (they start at just $250 per month).

Realtor Leads Pay at Closing

With the exception of certain advertising-based programs, most Revenos opportunities operate on a referral-fee-at-closing structure. Fees typically range from 25 percent to 50 percent, depending on the program and lead source.

Referral fees apply only when a transaction successfully closes. Agents retain full ownership of future client relationships that result from completed transactions, subject to standard brokerage referral rules.

Participation in Revenos programs does not alter an agent’s commission split, cap structure, or eligibility for other eXp Realty compensation programs.

What Agents Also Ask About Revenos Leads

Are Revenos leads exclusive to eXp agents?
Yes. Revenos programs are available only to licensed eXp Realty agents who meet eligibility requirements and complete any required training or certification. These referral and lead programs are operated internally by eXp Realty and are not accessible to agents affiliated with other brokerages.

Do agents pay upfront for Revenos leads?
Most Revenos programs operate on a referral-fee-at-closing model rather than upfront lead purchases. Agents typically pay a percentage only when a transaction successfully closes. Making It Rain is the primary exception, as it involves paid advertising spend rather than referral fees.

How quickly do Revenos leads convert in practice?
Conversion timelines vary by program and lead type. Some Revenos leads are transaction-ready, while others involve relocation, affinity, or long-term planning scenarios that may take months to convert. Agents should expect a mix of short-term and longer-cycle opportunities.

Does Revenos replace other lead sources at eXp Realty?
No. Revenos is designed to supplement, not replace, other lead sources such as organic marketing, sphere referrals, CRM-based lead capture, and additional eXp programs like Zoocasa or BoldTrail. Agents typically layer Revenos alongside other lead channels based on capacity.

Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty

Programs like eXp Realty’s Revenos Leads are designed to provide centralized, brokerage-supported lead opportunities, but they do not operate in isolation or replace the broader eXp Realty experience.

At eXp Realty, all agents receive the same core brokerage platform, including compliance, compensation, and access to company divisions. What differs is the sponsor ecosystem an agent aligns with.

The sponsor is selected during the application process, before most agents have used the brokerage’s systems, explored its tools, or seen how sponsorship works in real life. Knowing where sponsorship fits within eXp Realty’s overall structure helps agents view this decision in the right context.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Revenos mandatory for eXp agents?
No. All Revenos programs are optional and require agents to enroll and complete any required certifications. eXp Realty agents can operate entirely without Revenos and still access the brokerage’s core platform, tools, and other lead sources. Revenos exists as an additional lead option, not a requirement.

Do all eXp agents qualify for Revenos leads?
All licensed eXp agents may apply for Revenos programs, but eligibility varies by program. Some offerings require a minimum transaction history, specific certifications, or completion of training before access is granted. These requirements exist to ensure agents are prepared to handle the lead types involved.

Are Revenos referral fees negotiable?
No. Referral fees are set by each Revenos program and are disclosed in advance of participation. Fees are standardized to maintain consistency across agents and markets. Agents agree to the referral terms as part of enrollment, and fees are applied only when a transaction successfully closes.

Can agents participate in multiple Revenos programs at the same time?
Yes. Agents may participate in more than one Revenos program concurrently, provided they meet the certification, experience, and capacity requirements for each. Agents are expected to manage response times and follow-up effectively, as stacking programs without sufficient capacity can reduce conversion success.

Does Revenos availability vary by market?
Yes. Revenos lead volume and program availability depend on geography, partner relationships, and consumer demand. Some programs are more active in certain regions than others. Availability may change over time as new partnerships are added or demand shifts within specific markets.

Related eXp Realty Lead Guides

  • Zoocasa: Hot Leads Only Available at eXp Realty
  • Lead Generation Got Smarter: Meet eXp’s “My Link My Lead” Tool
  • Should Realtors Rent Leads or Own Their Pipeline?

title: How Collaboration, Events, and Global Connection Actually Work
excerpt: Learn how eXp Realty community actually works, from daily collaboration and EXPCON events to global locations and sponsor-led connection.
content:
Key Takeaway: eXp Realty community is not a vibe or a slogan. It is an operating layer built into the brokerage. Agents collaborate daily across markets, attend live events that actually drive production, and plug into a global network that makes a large brokerage feel personal. Community at eXp exists at three levels: daily collaboration, live events, and local connection. Smart Agent Alliance adds an additional layer of structured community on top.

TL;DR About eXp Realty Community

  • eXp agents collaborate instead of competing because the model removes local scarcity.
  • Daily collaboration happens inside eXp World, eXp Hub, and sponsor-led masterminds.
  • Live events like EXPCON and regional rallies turn online relationships into real-world momentum.
  • Local meetups and global locations keep the cloud brokerage grounded.
  • Smart Agent Alliance adds consistent, organized collaboration inside the larger eXp ecosystem.

Most brokerages talk about culture. Few design for it.

At many traditional firms, “community” means occasional office meetings, surface-level networking, or competition disguised as camaraderie. At eXp Realty, community is built into how agents work, learn, and grow. Collaboration is not optional or accidental. It is structural.

This page is the hub for our eXp Realty Community series. Below is a big-picture map of how collaboration, events, and global connection actually function at eXp, with links to deep-dive guides for each piece so you can explore what matters most to you.

Here’s your handy dandy index.

Table of Contents

TL;DR About eXp Realty Community
How eXp Realty Community Is Structured
Daily Collaboration Inside eXp Realty
Live Events and In-Person Connection
Local Connection Without Traditional Offices
Where Sponsorship Fits Into eXp Community
Why Realtors Need Community (and How to Find the Right One)
Smart Agent Alliance Disclosure

How eXp Realty Community Is Structured

eXp is a cloud brokerage, but that does not mean agents operate in isolation. Instead of one physical office, eXp built multiple overlapping community layers:

  • Daily collaboration through virtual platforms
  • Live events that create momentum and relationships
  • Local connection through meetups and global office access
  • Sponsor teams that make a large brokerage feel small

Each layer serves a different purpose, but together they create a working community that supports production, learning, and long-term growth.

Daily Collaboration Inside eXp Realty

The core of eXp Realty community is daily collaboration. Agents do not wait for annual conferences to connect. They collaborate every week, often every day. At a high level, eXp Realty community is built around daily collaboration. Agents do not wait for annual conferences to connect.

eXp World and eXp Hub

eXp World is the company’s virtual campus where agents attend live training, host meetings, and collaborate across markets. eXp Hub functions as the always-on communication layer, where agents share strategies, referrals, and real-time problem solving.

This combination replaces the traditional office model with something more scalable. Instead of learning only from the agents in your zip code, you learn from producers across the country and around the world.

For a full breakdown of how multi-agent collaboration works day to day, explore eXp Collaboration in our deep-dive guide.

Live Events and In-Person Connection

Online collaboration is powerful. Live events are where relationships deepen and momentum accelerates.

EXPCON and Major Company Events

EXPCON is eXp Realty’s flagship event. It brings agents together for multi-day training, strategy, leadership updates, and networking that actually leads to referrals and partnerships. This is where agents meet the people they have been collaborating with all year and turn digital relationships into real business.

Regional Rallies and Market-Specific Events

Between major conferences, eXp hosts regional rallies designed around local markets. These events focus on market-specific strategies, regional networking, and practical execution. They help bridge the gap between big-picture ideas and daily production.

Sponsor and Community Events

In addition to company-hosted events, some sponsor organizations host their own in-person gatherings. These are not required by eXp, but the best sponsor teams use them to strengthen connection, collaboration, and accountability.

To see how EXPCON, regional rallies, and sponsor-hosted events actually work, explore eXp Events in our full deep-dive guide.

Local Connection Without Traditional Offices

Even though eXp does not rely on brick-and-mortar offices, local connection still matters.

Agent-Led Local Meetups

Many eXp agents organize local meetups on their own. These range from casual coffee meetups to structured masterminds. Because the culture is collaborative rather than competitive, these gatherings tend to focus on sharing what works, not posturing.

Global Locations and Workspace Access

eXp agents also have access to physical meeting and workspace options through global office partnerships. This allows agents to meet clients, collaborate locally, or work while traveling, without being tied to a single office location.

You can explore how global access works and where agents can meet in person on the eXp Realty Locations page.

Where Sponsorship Fits Into eXp Community

Sponsorship at eXp exists alongside the brokerage, not above it. eXp provides the platform, systems, and global network. Sponsors determine how much additional community they create.

Many sponsors provide little or no ongoing connection. Some operate informal groups. A small number build structured communities with regular collaboration, training, and live interaction.

Smart Agent Alliance is one of those organized sponsor teams.

Why Realtors Need Community (and How to Find the Right One)

Many agents assume community will appear naturally once they join a brokerage, but most discover that proximity doesn’t equal connection. Without meaningful collaboration, even experienced agents end up solving problems in isolation, which accelerates burnout and stalls growth.

Explore why real estate community plays a critical role in retention, performance, and long-term success, and how agents can identify communities that actually support them rather than just surround them.

Smart Agent Alliance Disclosure

Smart Agent Alliance operates as an organized sponsor support structure within eXp Realty. Participation is optional and occurs only when an agent independently aligns with a Smart Agent Alliance–affiliated sponsor. Agents retain full independence and control of their businesses.

Full details are available on the Smart Agent Alliance eXp Realty sponsor page.

Frequently Asked Questions

How does a virtual real estate community support agent collaboration at eXp Realty?

A virtual real estate community allows agents to collaborate without geographic limits. At eXp Realty, agents connect through platforms like eXp World and eXp Hub to share referrals, attend training, ask questions, and collaborate across markets in real time.

Is the eXp Realty community only online?

No. While eXp Realty operates as a cloud brokerage, agents also connect through in-person events, regional meetups, masterminds, and national conferences. The community combines virtual collaboration with real-world relationship building.

Do eXp agents actually collaborate, or is community mostly marketing?

Collaboration at eXp Realty is built into daily operations. Agents regularly exchange referrals, participate in live training, and engage in sponsor-led groups designed for practical collaboration rather than marketing alone.

Are eXp events and community activities required for agents?

No. Participation in eXp Realty community events and activities is optional. Agents choose their level of involvement based on their goals, schedule, and preferred working style.

Do all eXp Realty sponsors provide the same level of community?

No. While eXp Realty provides core community platforms, the level of additional organization, leadership, and engagement varies by sponsor. Community support depends on the sponsor an agent aligns with.

How does Smart Agent Alliance differ from the general eXp Realty community?

Smart Agent Alliance operates as a sponsor-led layer within eXp Realty, providing organized leadership access, systems, and collaboration beyond the brokerage’s baseline community for agents who choose to participate.

title: Should Realtors Buy Leads: Rent Leads or Own Their Pipeline
excerpt: Should you rent leads vs own pipeline real estate? Learn why building your own database with Smart Agent Alliance creates freedom and sustainable growth at eXp.
content:
Key Takeaway: The decision between renting real estate leads and owning a lead pipeline affects cost control, conversion efficiency, and long-term business equity. Rented leads stop when payments end, while owned pipelines continue to generate repeat and referral activity over time. This distinction shapes sustainability, predictability, and how much control agents retain over future business growth.

TL;DR About Renting Leads vs Owning a Pipeline at eXp Realty

  • Renting leads provides short-term contact access but no lasting asset
  • Owned pipelines convert higher due to familiarity and trust
  • Paid lead flow stops immediately when spending stops
  • CRM-based pipelines create reusable, compounding value
  • eXp Realty provides CRM tools that support pipeline ownership
  • Sponsorship ecosystems can determine how effectively tools are used

If you’ve been spending thousands each month on Zillow, Realtor.com, or paid social leads, you’re not alone. Many agents operate on rented attention without realizing they are building someone else’s asset instead of their own. Over time, that distinction shows up in costs, stress, and sustainability. This article explains how renting leads versus owning your pipeline fits into the broader eXp Realty leads ecosystem available to eXp agents. Here’s your index:

Table of Contents

The Problem With Renting Real Estate Leads
The Value of Owning Your Pipeline
How eXp Realty Supports Pipeline Ownership
The Payoff: Freedom from Lead Dependency
What Agents Also Ask About Lead Generation

Frequently Asked Questions

Related eXp Realty Lead Guides

The Problem With Renting Real Estate Leads

Renting real estate leads means paying ongoing fees for temporary access to prospects that multiple agents contact simultaneously. Once payments stop, lead flow ends, leaving agents with no lasting database or business asset.

The problem with renting leads isn’t just the high cost; it’s the lack of control. You’re building someone else’s business while draining your own profit margin. Lead companies charge $50 to $300 per name, often reselling the same “hot” lead to five or more agents at once (see examples in 12 Real Estate Lead Generation Strategies). Lead companies charge $50 to $300 per name, often reselling the same “hot” lead to five or more agents at once. Conversion rates are typically low, requiring sustained spend and follow-up to generate results.

It’s a treadmill disguised as progress: to maintain income, you must keep paying. Miss one month, and your lead flow stops cold. There’s no equity, no residual value, and no future income once the spending ends. Lead providers design it this way; they want you dependent enough to stay subscribed forever.

The following example illustrates how some agents describe building and using an owned pipeline in practice. Take it from Mauricio who says that he built his business around consistency, not dependency. Adding just five people a day to his database turned into 50+ closings a year with no paid leads required.

Think of it like renting farmland: you pay for every harvest but never own the soil. You’re feeding someone else’s empire while hoping they’ll keep giving you access to the crops. Meanwhile, agents who own their databases are building generational wealth from their own fields.

The Value of Owning Your Pipeline

Owning your real estate pipeline means controlling lead flow through your own database, systems, and content rather than paying third-party providers. Owned pipelines generate higher-quality leads, consistent referrals, and long-term business equity you can grow or sell.

When you own your pipeline, every contact collected becomes part of a reusable database that supports repeat and referral activity over time. Unlike rented leads that vanish the moment you stop paying, owned databases compound in value as your relationships grow stronger.

Here’s the math traditional brokerages don’t teach: a $3,000 monthly lead budget equals $36,000 a year, money that disappears with no residual value. Put that same effort into building a CRM-driven pipeline, and you create an asset that delivers leads for decades. Every past client becomes a future closing, every email sequence a digital salesperson working 24/7.

Owned pipelines also mean freedom from competition. You’re not racing to call a lead five seconds faster than ten other agents. You’re communicating with people who already know, like, and trust you. These are clients who choose you because you’ve built authority and familiarity over time.

And let’s be honest, owning your pipeline feels good. It’s like finally buying your dream home after years of renting apartments with mystery stains and thin walls. You’re no longer at the mercy of someone else’s system. You own the foundation, the roof, and the mailbox where all the good referrals show up.

How eXp Realty Supports Pipeline Ownership

eXp Realty supports pipeline ownership through built-in technology, CRM tools, and global referral networks that help agents build sustainable lead systems they control (see eXp’s “My Link My Lead” tool). The model empowers agents to create repeat and referral business instead of relying on purchased leads.

Most brokerages talk about “support,” but what they really mean is “you’re on your own with a desk fee.” eXp Realty actually delivers tools that make ownership possible. Every agent starts with a CRM choice (BoldTrail, Lofty, or Cloze) CRM, access to top training for getting your own leads, and easy referral tools. All is already included in the $85 monthly brokerage fee. That’s your digital command center for nurturing leads, automating follow-ups, and tracking your database from anywhere.

The secret sauce? eXp’s model rewards ownership at every level. Agents who help others build pipelines share in the company’s revenue through revenue share, and just doing deals earns EXPI stock awards, real equity in the brokerage itself. This alignment creates a culture of collaboration, not competition. And, that’s very different then your local brokerage office, where agents may share a bit of good information but they hide the best tips.

In short, eXp Realty gives you the tools to own your pipeline instead of leasing someone else’s. Think of it like moving from paying rent to joining a co-op: you’re not just using the system, you’re profiting from it.

The Payoff: Freedom from Lead Dependency

Reducing reliance on paid lead providers allows agents to shift toward database-driven business models. Owning your database creates predictable income, higher ROI, and long-term stability that grows even when marketing budgets shrink.

When agents stop renting leads and start owning their pipeline, everything changes. Stress drops, costs shrink, and business becomes predictable again. Instead of gambling $5,000 a month hoping for a few conversions, you’re running a system that compounds. Your CRM turns into a gold mine and each conversation today becomes tomorrow’s closing.

Agents who shift to ownership see the difference almost immediately. The first few months save thousands in expenses. Within a year, referrals and repeat clients replace cold prospects entirely. And by year three, you’ve built a self-sustaining lead engine that generates income even when you’re not prospecting daily.

That’s the real definition of leverage: your time and relationships working for you. Owned pipelines convert at 5–10x higher rates, cost pennies to maintain, and build business equity that can be sold or passed on later. Lead renters keep hustling for the next batch of internet strangers; pipeline owners wake up to new opportunities already in their inbox.

Owning your lead system is the business equivalent of planting fruit trees instead of buying fruit. The first harvest takes effort, but once it starts producing, it never stops. And best of all? You never have to worry about someone else turning off your water supply.

What Agents Also Ask About Lead Generation

How long does it take to replace paid leads with an owned pipeline?

Most agents who implement consistent follow-up and CRM automation see meaningful replacement of paid leads within 90 to 120 days. The timeline depends on contact volume, frequency of outreach, and database organization. Owned pipelines grow through accumulated conversations, not instant volume, so momentum increases gradually rather than all at once.

Do I need a large social media following to build an owned pipeline?

No. Pipeline strength is driven by relationship depth, not audience size. A database of 100 to 200 engaged contacts can outperform thousands of unengaged followers. Consistent follow-up, relevance, and segmentation matter more than platform reach when building repeat and referral-based lead flow.

Can agents use paid leads while building an owned pipeline?

Yes. Many agents use paid leads as a short-term supplement while transitioning to ownership. The key is transferring those contacts into a CRM, tagging them properly, and nurturing them over time. This approach turns temporary access into long-term database growth rather than isolated transactions.

Why do owned pipelines typically convert at higher rates?

Owned pipelines convert better because trust accumulates over time. Contacts recognize the agent’s name, communication style, and expertise before a transaction need arises. Familiarity reduces friction, increases response rates, and shortens decision cycles compared to cold or shared paid leads.

Frequently Asked Questions

How long does it usually take to stop relying on purchased leads entirely?

Agents who consistently work their CRM and follow structured nurture plans often phase out purchased leads within three to six months. Results depend on contact frequency, database size, and follow-up quality. The transition is incremental rather than instant, with overlap periods where both systems run concurrently.

What if I am starting with little or no database at all?

Agents can begin by importing past contacts, phone lists, email connections, open house leads, and social connections into a CRM. Every interaction becomes a starting point. Over time, new contacts are added through referrals, website forms, and client introductions, creating a controllable pipeline.

Does owning a pipeline require more daily effort than buying leads?

Initial setup requires more intentional work, but long-term effort is typically lower. Automation handles much of the follow-up once systems are in place. Compared to managing multiple paid platforms and chasing cold contacts, owned pipelines reduce ongoing workload and improve response quality.

How much does it cost to build an owned pipeline at eXp Realty?

eXp Realty includes CRM access and core technology in its standard monthly fee. Additional systems may be optional depending on sponsor alignment. Agents are not required to purchase third-party lead platforms to build or maintain an owned pipeline within the brokerage structure.

Can paid leads be converted into owned database contacts?

Yes. Purchased leads can be imported into an agent’s CRM, tagged, and nurtured through long-term communication plans. Once stored and managed inside the agent’s system, those contacts become part of the owned pipeline rather than isolated paid opportunities.

What is the most common mistake agents make when building pipelines?

Inconsistent follow-up. Most transactions occur after multiple touches, not the first contact. Without structured reminders or automation, leads fall through gaps. CRM systems exist to prevent this, but they must be actively used to protect long-term pipeline value.

  • Making It Rain: eXp Realty’s PPC Leads That Works

title: How My Link My Lead Functions at eXp Realty
excerpt: My Link My Lead helps eXp Realty agents generate leads, track referrals, and grow their business with ease using this exclusive tool.
content:
Key Takeaways: My Link My Lead is eXp Realty’s link-based lead attribution tool that allows agents to capture and track consumer activity nationwide using a personalized URL. It turns everyday link sharing into attributable lead generation and referral opportunities without relying on ads.

TL;DR About My Link My Lead at eXp Realty

  • Agents receive a personalized tracking link connected to eXp’s national property search site
  • Leads who click the link are automatically attributed to the agent
  • Works across all U.S. markets, not limited to the agent’s service area
  • Enables fast referral routing to other eXp agents when leads are out of area

It gives eXp Realty agents a personalized tracking link tied to eXp’s national search portal. eXp Realty is dedicated to making My Link My Lead even better. They’re dedicated to making My Link My Lead even better. This means they’re listening to feedback from agents like you. Getting Started with My Link My Lead

Signing Up for Early Access

Let’s go straight to the start. If you want to be at the front of the line, sign up for early access to “My Link My Lead.” This is your best opportunity to get a leg up! Here’s how you do it: head to the official website, look for the early access banner, and follow the simple sign-up steps. It’s about as easy as it gets. Now, why the rush? As one of the early ones, you can check out the tool’s features before anyone else. You get that early access so you can start getting those valuable leads. Early engagement is really what it’s about. The sooner you start, the faster you boost your lead-gen potential. So don’t dilly-dally — get your space today.

Creating and Sharing Links

Let’s create them and share links. This is the fertile ground, the area where the magic takes place. You’ll create a custom URL specifically for your prospects. It’s essential to get these links out there to a broader audience. Your best friend here is consistency — regular sharing keeps you top-of-mind with your prospects. Don’t be afraid to try multiple platforms. Try them all and see which works best for you.

Utilizing the Referral Tool

The referral tool inside of “My Link My Lead” is a motherlode for putting together a strong referral network. Start by sending your referral links to other agents and ask them to do the same. This creates a net of connections, opening additional doors to leads. Timely referrals are what you need to close deals, so don’t sit on your hands. Get into that referral process — you’ll find every bit of it to be worth your while. Remember, this tool allows you to reach specific leads — even those searching for homes in all 50 states. You can refer leads beyond your service area by sharing links to the national search portal. It’s a win-win!

What Agents Also Ask About My Link My Lead

Is My Link My Lead a replacement for paid lead programs?

No. My Link My Lead is designed to complement paid lead programs rather than replace them. It focuses on attribution, tracking, and organic lead capture from content, links, and shared resources. The tool does not generate demand on its own but helps agents retain ownership of interest they already create.

Who benefits most from using My Link My Lead?

It is especially useful for agents building visibility across multiple markets, social platforms, or referral networks where maintaining consistent attribution is more difficult with standard links.

Does using My Link My Lead require actively managing referrals?

How does My Link My Lead differ from a normal IDX or website link?
Unlike standard IDX or website links, My Link My Lead preserves agent attribution across browsing sessions and geographic areas. This continuity allows agents to maintain ownership of interest even when users return later or explore listings outside the agent’s immediate market.

Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty

Do all eXp agents receive a personalized My Link My Lead URL?
Yes. Eligible eXp Realty agents receive access to a personalized My Link My Lead URL through the eXp platform. This link is tied to the agent’s account and is designed to preserve attribution when consumers interact with listings or content shared by that agent.

Does My Link My Lead generate leads automatically?
No. My Link My Lead does not generate demand on its own. It tracks and attributes activity from links that the agent actively shares. The tool captures interest created by the agent’s marketing or content rather than functioning as a standalone lead generation system.

Can agents opt out of using My Link My Lead?
Yes. There is no requirement to use My Link My Lead. Agents may choose not to use the tool if it does not align with their business model, marketing approach, or lead strategy. Opting out does not affect access to other eXp Realty tools or programs.

Does My Link My Lead replace a CRM or lead management system?
No. My Link My Lead functions as an attribution and routing layer, not a CRM. Agents still rely on a customer relationship management system to handle follow-up, communication, task management, and long-term relationship tracking with prospects and clients.

title: How eXp Realty Teams for Agents Are Structured
excerpt: Understand how eXp Realty teams operate in 2026, including Full Cap, Half Cap, and Quarter Cap structures, commission splits, and cap rules.
content:
Key Takeaway: eXp Realty teams include multiple organized structures that affect commission splits, company dollar caps, and ICON eligibility. Understanding the difference between sponsor teams and organized teams clarifies how compensation and qualification rules apply before joining or forming a team.

TL;DR About eXp Realty Teams

  • eXp Realty supports multiple team structures, each with different caps and requirements
  • Sponsor teams and organized teams serve different functions and are not mutually exclusive
  • Commission is split between team members and leaders, then paid to eXp individually
  • Reduced caps come with mandatory minimum team leader splits
  • ICON eligibility depends on whether an agent remains on the full cap
  • Teams can operate across states with additional compliance steps
  • 2026 update renamed and adjusted organized team structures

Table of Contents

TL;DR About eXp Realty Teams
The Two Types of eXp Realty Teams
What Is a Real Estate Team?
Exploring eXp Realty Team Structures
How Do eXp Realty Commission Splits Work on a Team?
How Team Leader Splits and Brokerage Fees Apply
Can You Add Team Members in Another State?
What Agents Also Ask About eXp Realty Teams
Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty
Frequently Asked Questions
Related eXp Realty Fit Guides

Real estate teams can accelerate growth, but the structure you choose determines how commissions, caps, fees, and recognition programs actually work. At eXp Realty, teams are formalized with specific rules that differ from both traditional brokerages and informal agent partnerships.

In 2026, eXp Realty updated the naming conventions and minimum split requirements for its organized team structures. For a detailed breakdown of production thresholds, blended split rules, cap options, and company dollar requirements, see our detailed eXp Realty Teams 2026 structure guide.

This article explains how eXp Realty teams fit into the broader eXp Realty Fit ecosystem available to eXp agents. The sections below outline team types, cap mechanics, commission splits, and 2026 structural updates:

The Two Types of eXp Realty Teams

Before examining commission splits and formal team structures, it is important to clarify that the term “eXp Realty team” can refer to two distinct arrangements within the brokerage. Understanding the difference helps agents evaluate how structure, compensation, and support function in each context.

eXp Sponsor Teams

At eXp Realty, sponsorship is tied to an individual agent rather than a formally recognized team. Some sponsors collaborate informally with other sponsors and may collectively refer to themselves as a “sponsor team.” However, these groups are not officially designated or regulated as teams by eXp Realty.

When an agent joins eXp Realty, they name a single individual sponsor, even if that sponsor participates in a broader collaborative network. Sponsors who collaborate may share systems, meetings, or educational resources. Any additional support provided through these informal groups is optional, varies by sponsor, and operates independently from the brokerage’s required services and compliance structure.

eXp Real Estate Organized Teams

This section focuses on organized real estate teams at eXp Realty. Organized teams are formal business structures consisting of a team leader and one or more licensed team members operating under a written team agreement. That agreement defines roles, compensation arrangements, responsibilities, and operational standards.

Organized teams function as structured business entities within the brokerage. Commission splits are predetermined, duties are clearly assigned, and internal systems are established to support growth and operational efficiency. eXp Realty does not manage or intervene in internal team agreements, which makes clear documentation and defined expectations essential.

Participation in Both Structures

Agents may participate in both a sponsor team and an organized team structure simultaneously. Each serves a separate function within the brokerage. Sponsorship relates to revenue share alignment and optional support, while organized teams define business operations, commission splits, and day-to-day workflow.

What Is a Real Estate Team?

A real estate team is a group of licensed agents and support professionals operating within a defined structure to manage transactions and client service. Responsibilities are typically divided among team members to improve efficiency and consistency.

For example, some teams assign buyer specialists to manage purchase transactions, while listing agents focus on pricing strategy and seller representation. Transaction coordinators oversee compliance and documentation, often using brokerage platforms such as SkySlope. Administrative staff may manage scheduling, marketing coordination, and CRM systems.

Not all real estate teams follow the same model. Some operate with clearly defined specialization and hierarchy, while others maintain more flexible collaboration structures in which agents share leads and resources but handle transactions independently.

Regardless of format, the purpose of a real estate team is to create operational efficiency, provide structured client service, and support business scalability within the brokerage framework.

Exploring eXp Realty Team Structures

eXp Realty recognizes four formal organized team structures. Each structure defines how agents collaborate, how company dollar caps apply, and how compensation arrangements are structured within the brokerage framework.

Understanding the purpose of each structure helps agents evaluate how they want to operate before forming or joining a team.

Domestic Partnerships

A Domestic Partnership is designed for two legally married agents or partners in a legally recognized union who operate their business jointly. This structure allows both partners to function within a shared cap framework under defined brokerage rules.

Domestic Parnterships are limited to two agents and operate differently from larger organized teams. Because of the shared structure, certain recognition and cap mechanics apply collectively rather than individually.

Full Cap Teams

A Full Cap Team is an organized team structure in which all agents remain on the standard individual company dollar cap. There are no reduced cap options within this structure.

This model allows agents to collaborate operationally while maintaining their individual cap progression and eligibility for recognition programs. Compensation splits between team members and leaders are defined by the team agreement, while brokerage-level company dollar and transaction fees are applied individually.

Full Cap Teams are often used by groups that want operational alignment without altering the standard cap framework.

Half Cap Teams

Half Cap Teams allow team members to elect a reduced company dollar cap option. When a reduced cap is selected, brokerage policy requires a defined minimum compensation structure between the team member and team leader.

This structure is typically used by teams that prioritize centralized lead flow, role specialization, or operational leverage, where team members trade a higher internal split for a lower brokerage cap.

Agents who remain on the standard cap within this structure retain eligibility for brokerage recognition programs, while reduced-cap participation may affect eligibility.

Quarter Cap Teams

Quarter Cap Teams are designed for larger, high-production teams operating within a more structured compensation framework. Members may elect reduced cap options under brokerage policy, which requires defined minimum team leader compensation standards.

Because this structure includes additional qualification requirements and compliance oversight, it is generally used by teams operating at significant scale.

Quarter Cap Teams provide flexibility in cap elections while maintaining structured internal compensation rules that align with brokerage policy.

Each organized team structure operates within eXp Realty’s broader brokerage model. Sponsorship alignment, revenue share reporting, and brokerage-level company dollar percentages remain separate from internal team agreements.

For a detailed breakdown of production qualifications, blended split requirements, cap election mechanics, and structural updates, see our full eXp Realty Teams 2026 structure guide.

How Do eXp Realty Commission Splits Work on a Team?

Understanding how commission is calculated on an organized eXp Realty team is essential before joining. When you are part of an organized eXp Realty team, commission is typically divided in two stages. First, the gross commission is split between the team leader and the team member according to the team agreement. Then eXp Realty applies its company dollar and transaction fees to each agent’s individual share.

This structure reflects how organized teams operate within eXp Realty’s brokerage model. Understanding this sequence helps agents evaluate how much of their commission they ultimately retain.

How Commissions Are Calculated on eXp Teams

Here’s the general order of operations:

  • Any outside referrals owed to other brokers come off the top.
  • The remaining gross commission is split between the team leader and team member based on your agreement (commonly 50/50).
  • Then, eXp’s company dollar (the 20% split up to your cap) and transaction fees are deducted from each agent’s share individually.
  • Fees like the broker review fee, capped transaction fee, and risk management fee are per transaction, not per agent, so they’re usually split 50/50.

Real Example: $10,000 Gross Commission Deal

Assumptions:

  • 50/50 split between the team leader and team member
  • Team leader is capped
  • Team member is not capped

Let’s say your team just closed a deal for $10,000.

  • $5,000 goes to the Team Leader
  • $5,000 goes to the Team Member

Team Leader’s Cut (Capped):

  • Start with $5,000
  • Subtract 50% of capped transaction fee ($250)  $5,000 – $125 = $4,875
  • Subtract 50% of broker review fee ($25)  $4,875 – $12.50 = $4,862.50
  • Subtract 50% of risk management fee ($60)  $4,862.50 – $30 = $4,832.50 net

Team Member’s Cut (Uncapped):

Start with $5,000

  • Subtract 20% to eXp ($1,000)  $4,000
  • Subtract 50% of broker review fee ($25)) $4000 – $12.50 = $3,987.50
  • Subtract 50% of risk management fee ($60) $3,987.50 – $30 = $3,957.50 net

How Team Leader Splits and Brokerage Fees Apply

When an agent participates in a reduced cap structure under a Half Cap Team or Quarter Cap Team, the team leader receives the required minimum average split defined by current team policy. This team split is separate from eXp Realty’s company dollar and transaction fees, which are applied individually to each agent’s share of the commission.

In other words, compensation is first divided according to the team agreement, and then brokerage-level company dollar and applicable transaction fees are deducted from each agent independently.

Transaction-related fees, such as broker review fees, risk management fees, and capped transaction fees, are assessed per transaction and are typically allocated between the team leader and team member according to the team agreement.

Understanding this order of operations allows agents to accurately evaluate net compensation under an organized team structure.

Can You Add Team Members in Another State?

Organized eXp Realty teams may include agents licensed in different states, provided brokerage compliance requirements are met.

When adding a team member licensed in another state, the team must ensure proper licensing alignment, supervision structure, and transaction processing compliance within that jurisdiction. Real estate regulations vary by state, and additional documentation or approval steps may be required before the agent can be formally added to the team.

Before onboarding an out-of-state team member, teams should coordinate with eXp Team Services to confirm licensing compatibility, roster updates, and state-specific requirements.

There is an important distinction for Domestic Teams. Because Domestic Teams operate under a shared cap and joint structure, both partners must hold the appropriate license in any state where transactions are conducted. If one partner is not licensed in that state, commissions may require referral processing rather than direct payment.

Cross-state team expansion is permitted under eXp Realty’s team policies, but compliance review is required to ensure transactions are processed in accordance with state law and brokerage standards.

What Agents Also Ask About eXp Realty Teams

Are eXp Realty teams required to operate under one structure?

eXp Realty recognizes multiple organized team structures, including Domestic Teams, Full Cap Teams, Half Cap Teams, and Quarter Cap Teams. Each structure has defined production requirements, cap mechanics, reduced-cap options, and minimum average team leader split requirements. Teams must operate within one of these formally recognized structures and comply with the associated eligibility and documentation standards.

How do eXp Realty teams compare to traditional brokerage teams?

eXp Realty teams operate without physical office overhead and within standardized brokerage rules. Unlike traditional brokerages, commission caps, reduced-cap options, and ICON eligibility are clearly defined, while internal team agreements control splits, roles, and daily operations independently.

Which type of agents seek to join an eXp Realty team?

Agents who prefer operating within a defined structure often explore organized teams. Teams may include shared lead systems, role specialization, and predefined compensation splits. Agents who prefer independent production without shared compensation arrangements may choose to remain outside a team structure.

Does joining an eXp Realty team limit future career paths?

Joining an eXp Realty team does not lock agents into a permanent structure. Agents may later transition to solo production, leadership roles, or other teams, provided contractual obligations are met and brokerage compliance requirements are followed.

Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty

eXp Realty teams are designed to address agent use cases such as shared lead flow, mentorship, operational leverage, and faster scaling through collaboration, but they do not operate in isolation or replace the broader brokerage experience.

At eXp Realty, all agents receive the same core brokerage platform, including compliance, compensation, and access to company divisions. What differs is the sponsor ecosystem an agent aligns with.

The sponsor is selected during the application process, before most agents have used the brokerage’s systems, explored its tools, or seen how sponsorship works in real life. Knowing where sponsorship fits within eXp Realty’s overall structure helps agents view this decision in the right context.

Frequently Asked Questions

1. Are eXp Realty teams required?

eXp Realty does not require agents to join an organized team structure. Agents may operate independently under the brokerage model or choose to participate in a formal team. Team participation is optional and depends on how an agent prefers to structure production, collaboration, and internal business operations.

2. What is the difference between a sponsor team and an organized team?

A sponsor team refers to an informal collaboration group aligned through sponsorship and revenue share. An organized team is a formally registered business structure within eXp Realty that defines commission splits, operational roles, and responsibilities under a written team agreement recognized by the brokerage.

3. How do commission splits typically work on an eXp Realty team?

On an organized team, gross commission is first divided according to the written team agreement between the team leader and the team member. After that division, eXp Realty applies company dollar and transaction-related fees individually to each agent’s portion of the commission.

4. Does joining an organized team change my sponsor?

Joining an organized team does not change an agent’s sponsor. Sponsorship is designated during enrollment and relates to revenue share alignment. Organized team participation governs operational structure and commission agreements, but it does not alter sponsorship designation or tier placement.

5. Can I leave an eXp Realty team later?

Agents may leave an organized team subject to the terms of their written team agreement and brokerage compliance policies. Transitioning out of a team does not automatically change sponsorship alignment, though contractual obligations and notice provisions may apply depending on the agreement.

6. Can eXp Realty teams operate across multiple states?

Organized eXp Realty teams may include agents licensed in different states, provided brokerage compliance standards and state licensing requirements are satisfied. Additional documentation or approval steps may be required before transactions can be processed under the expanded structure.

Success Lending eXp is an optional mortgage solution available through eXp Realty Solutions. It operates as a joint venture lending platform designed to coordinate communication and transaction workflows between agents, loan officers, and clients without requiring exclusivity or changes to an agent’s existing lender relationships.

This isn’t just another lender. Success Lending integrates seamlessly with the eXp Realty platform with the goal of giving agents and clients an improved home financing experience. For eXp agents, Success Lending is just one part of eXp Solutions for agents to help their clients.

Overview of Mortgage Solutions at Success Lending eXp

Success Lending eXp offers a wide variety of loan programs designed to meet the needs of nearly any homebuyer. Their lineup includes:

  • Conventional Loans
  • VA Loans
  • FHA Loans
  • First-Time Homebuyer Programs
  • Jumbo Loans

…and more.

Clients may use a digital application process that supports document submission and status updates during the mortgage process. Success Lending eXp matches each client with the right loan for their needs, keeping long-term financial health in mind.

Where Does Success Lending Operate?

Success Lending eXp operates in multiple states and they’re still expanding. States like: Alabama, Arizona, California, Colorado, Connecticut, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Iowa, Louisiana, Maryland, Michigan, Minnesota, Nebraska, New Jersey, North Carolina, Ohio, Oklahoma, Oregon, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, West Virginia, and Wisconsin. Whether your client is buying locally or relocating across state lines, this lender has the regional expertise to navigate state-specific regulations and deliver proven mortgage solutions. They’re here to help you and your clients get to the closing table—no matter where “home” happens to be.

Leadership and Team Expertise

Success Lending eXp is staffed by licensed mortgage professionals responsible for originating and managing loans within the platform. Loan officer experience, availability, and responsiveness may vary by state and transaction complexity.

How Success Lending Integrates Within eXp Realty Solutions

The relationship between eXp Realty and Success Lending is structured as a strategic integration within the eXp Realty Solutions ecosystem. This integration is intended to support coordination between real estate and lending workflows, allowing agents and loan officers to align communication and transaction processes during home purchase and financing activities.

This isn’t just about streamlining the mortgage process. By teaming up with Success Lending eXp, agents can offer their clients an all-in-one solution that simplifies everything from showing the home to closing the loan.

This partnership reflects an approach focused on aligning real estate and mortgage services within a shared operational framework rather than redefining industry standards.

How the Success Lending eXp Partnership Benefits Real Estate Agents

Agents who choose to use Success Lending may experience more centralized communication with loan officers, increased visibility into lending timelines, and familiarity with eXp Realty transaction workflows. Participation is optional and does not alter commission structure, caps, or sponsorship placement.

Get More with eXp Realty’s Preferred Partner Program

As part of eXp Realty’s Preferred Partner Program, Success Lending eXp gives agents VIP access to even more tools and training:

Exclusive Digital Training & Workshops

Stay up-to-date with Success Lending’s expert-led sessions on mortgages, market trends, and best practices.

Marketing Support & Resources

Tap into customizable templates, co-branded materials, and joint marketing strategies—just like in these proven real estate marketing assets tailored for eXp agents—to promote your services and attract more buyers.

Streamlined Communication Channels

Enjoy faster, direct access to Success Lending’s loan officers and support staff—helping you get answers (and updates) quickly.

This program is structured to provide eXp Realty agents with access to tools, training resources, and coordinated support that may be used to manage client interactions and transaction workflows, while allowing agents to remain focused on their core real estate activities.

How Coordinated Lending And Agent Collaboration Works In Practice

Some eXp Realty agents choose to work with Success Lending eXp loan officers as part of a coordinated transaction workflow. This collaboration focuses on aligning communication between real estate and lending teams, which can help clarify timelines, documentation requirements, and next steps during a transaction.

Loan officers working within Success Lending may coordinate directly with agents to review client financing scenarios, explain available loan programs, and align lending options with transaction details. This approach is often evaluated by agents working with first-time buyers, repeat clients, or transactions involving more complex financing considerations.

In addition to transaction coordination, eXp Realty agents may participate in the optional Success Lending eXp referral program. Agents who refer qualified loan officers may be eligible for referral-based compensation, including potential bonuses subject to program terms, which have been advertised at amounts up to $10,000. Participation is optional and operates separately from eXp Realty’s commission structure and revenue share model.

Some agents also reference access to shared tools, educational resources, and training materials when evaluating integrated lending options. These resources are intended to support agent understanding of mortgage processes and communication rather than replace independent lender relationships.

Overall, coordinated lending options are typically assessed as part of a broader decision about transaction management style, communication preferences, and how agents choose to structure client support during the financing process.

What Agents Also Ask About Success Lending eXp

Is Success Lending eXp required for eXp Realty agents?

No. Success Lending eXp is optional. Agents may use it, work with outside lenders, or maintain existing lending relationships. Choosing not to use Success Lending has no impact on commission splits, caps, sponsorship placement, or standing with eXp Realty.

How does Success Lending compare to using a personal or local lender?

Success Lending functions as an optional lending solution within eXp Realty Solutions, with an emphasis on communication coordination and workflow familiarity. Agents who already have trusted lenders may continue using them, while others may value the integrated support structure offered by Success Lending.

Who is Success Lending eXp best suited for?

It is often useful for agents who want tighter transaction coordination, work across multiple states, or prefer a lending option familiar with eXp Realty workflows. It may be less relevant for agents with long-standing lender partnerships they prefer to maintain.

Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty

Success Lending eXp is designed to help reduce one of the most common transaction friction points, lending delays and communication breakdowns, but it does not operate in isolation or replace the broader brokerage experience.

At eXp Realty, all agents receive the same core brokerage platform, including compliance, compensation, and access to company divisions. What differs is the sponsor ecosystem an agent aligns with.

The sponsor is selected during the application process, before most agents have used the brokerage’s systems, explored its tools, or seen how sponsorship works in real life. Knowing where sponsorship fits within eXp Realty’s overall structure helps agents view this decision in the right context.

Frequently Asked Questions

Do eXp Realty agents have to refer clients to Success Lending?

No. Referring clients to Success Lending is optional. eXp Realty agents may continue working with any lender they choose, including personal or local lending partners. Using or not using Success Lending has no impact on commission splits, caps, sponsorship placement, or an agent’s standing with the brokerage.

Does using Success Lending affect commission splits or caps?

No. Participation in Success Lending does not change an agent’s commission split, cap structure, revenue share eligibility, or other compensation terms. Lending usage is separate from the brokerage’s financial and compensation models and does not alter how agents are paid.

Is Success Lending available in every state?

No. Success Lending operates in multiple states and continues to expand, but availability depends on state licensing and lending regulations. Agents should confirm whether Success Lending operates in the specific state where a transaction is taking place before referring a client.

Does Success Lending replace other eXp Realty solutions or tools?

No. Success Lending functions alongside other eXp Realty Solutions and does not replace brokerage compliance systems, transaction management platforms, or outside vendors. Agents may use Success Lending selectively while continuing to rely on other tools and service providers.

  • eXp Realty Healthcare: Save Up to 80% on Premium Care!
  • Curbio + eXp Realty: The Hassle-Free Way to Boost Listings
